description: Tamil kingdom controlling spice trade routes and ports on the Malabar coast.
citations: Sangam literature; Periplus of the Erythraean Sea
notes: Associated with early St Thomas Christian tradition.

description: Powerful trading kingdom connecting Africa, Arabia, and the Mediterranean.
notes: Adopted Christianity in 4th century under King Ezana.

description: Armenian kingdom that became the first state to adopt Christianity as official religion.
Client kingdom contested between Rome and Parthia/Sassanids.
Eastern Georgian kingdom influenced by Rome and Persia.
notes: Christianized in 4th century under King Mirian III.

description: Local polities without centralized empire-level authority.
Arab kingdom controlling caravan trade routes before Roman annexation.
Breakaway empire ruled by Odaenathus and Queen Zenobia.
Region contested between Roman and Parthian powers, including Osrhoene and upper Mesopotamia.
notes: Repeated wars over Armenia and Mesopotamia.

description: Iranian empire rivaling Rome and controlling major Silk Road routes.
Boundary between Roman and Sassanian empires.
notes: Crucial for spread of Eastern Christianity.

description: Major Mediterranean empire under which early Christianity developed.
notes: Divided into eastern and western halves in late antiquity.

description: Iranian empire that succeeded the Parthians; chief rival of Rome; the dominant state within which the Church of the East developed and faced periodic persecution under Shapur I and II.
notes: Major context for Mesopotamian and eastern Christianity.
